name: Chocolate Curls
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Family Circle, vol. 105 no. 3, 2/1/93
Make 2 aluminum-foil boxes for dark curls and 1 box for white. For each, fold an 8x5 inch piece of foil in half to make a 5x4 inch rectangle. Fold long sides in twice to make a half-inch stand-up edge. Fold short ends to make a half-inch deep box (Box should be no wider than the length of the blade of a swivel-bladed peeler). For dark curls, melt semisweet chocolate chips and vegetable shortening in a saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly. Pour into 2 foil boxes. For white curls, melt white candy bar and vegetable shortening in a small saucepan, stirring. Pour into foil box. Spread any remaining chocolate in a thin layer on a piece of foil. Refrigerate at least 1 hour. Fold back foil edges. Let stand at room temperature for 10 minutes. To make curls, run the peeler along the length of the chocolate bars. Use the back of a spoon or edge of a metal spatula to pull curls from the thin layer of chocolate on foil. Transfer curls to cake with wooden pick.
